rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical vicinal amidoselenation of unactivated aliphatic alkenes with diaryl diselenides and acetonitrile via a tandem radical selenation/oxidative Ritter reaction
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of diphenyl diselenide to a diselenide radical cation that fragments to PhSe+ and PhSe•; the alkyl radical formed after PhSe• addition to the alkene is further oxidized at the anode to a selenonium cation, while protons are reduced at the cathode to H2
electron_flow: At the anode, Ph2Se2 is oxidized to its radical cation which fragments into PhSe+ and PhSe•; PhSe• adds to cyclohexene to give a β-selenoalkyl radical, which is then oxidized at the anode to a selenonium cation. Acetonitrile attacks this cation to form a nitrilium ion that is trapped by water, deprotonates/isomerizes to the amide product, while H+ is reduced to H2 at the cathode.
rate_determining_step: Nucleophilic attack of acetonitrile on the selenonium ion (TS4, ΔG‡ ≈ 21.12 kcal/mol) is the highest barrier on the productive pathway

probe_reagents.name: BHT (2,6-di-tert-butyl-4-methylphenol)
probe_reagents.observation: Yield suppressed to 55% (2 eq) and 20% (4 eq), indicating radical involvement

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Complete inhibition (trace product) with 2 eq; PhSe-DPE adduct detected by HRMS (m/z 337.0483)

probe_reagents.name: phenylselenenyl chloride (PhSeCl)
probe_reagents.observation: No product at 65 °C/5 h without oxidant; only 32% with (NH4)2S2O8; 15% under electrochemical conditions — suggests ionic PhSe+ pathway is inefficient and radical pathway dominates

probe_reagents.name: D2O / anhydrous conditions
probe_reagents.observation: Under dry MeCN/N2 only 43% yield obtained, confirming water is the oxygen source for the amide group

probe_reagents.name: selenonium cation 4a and PhSe-DPE adduct 4b
probe_reagents.observation: Both detected by HRMS (239.0332 and 337.0483), confirming selenonium and selenyl radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry
probe_reagents.observation: Cyclohexene is not oxidizable; Ph2Se2 shows Eox = 1.70 V; with TsOH the second oxidation peak of the mixture shifts from 2.36 to 2.10 V, showing acid promotes the electrochemical step

notes: Authors explicitly rule out a purely ionic PhSe+ pathway (no reaction with PhSeCl alone) in favor of a radical addition by PhSe• followed by anodic oxidation to the selenonium cation (radical-polar crossover). DFT shows the singlet selenonium 1IM2 is strongly favored over triplet 3IM2 (−35.93 vs −1.66 kcal/mol). Hydrogen gas at the cathode confirmed by detection experiment. Water (adventitious from solvent/air) is the source of the amide oxygen via Ritter-type trapping.
